storyInfo: A once glorious studio is facing imminent closure. Tippi says her goodbyes to her colleagues, who have no choice but to leave Wrankwood. Before they part ways, however, the three of them sneak into the warehouse to have a last look at something they signed many years ago.

attributes.content: Founded over a decade ago, Excelsior Studios has spent more than ten years building its business and has produced many popular late-night movies.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: At our peak, we were shooting day-in, day-out. Every midnight screening at every theater was one of ours.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: However, ticket sales have been dropping these past two years. We've had more than a few box-office busts.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Even soap opera directors are refusing to work with us now. They say we're an 'unserious studio' that 'only produces boring gore.'
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: And it's hard to deny that our management is as lacking as our funds. Truth is, there's no way we'll ever be making another movie.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Even I'm forced to admit that no one wants gory, violent films these days. We've lost our audience.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: I've put my heart and soul into our work, and I know you all have too. However, today I regret to inform you that...
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Excelsior Studios will be shutting down next week.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Ugh—all that melodrama, and in the end they aren't giving us a penny in severance! If they don't wanna pay us, then just say so!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: How are they supposed to pay us if they're bankrupt?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: You bought that nonsense? He's shuffling money from one hand to the other as we speak, getting ready to set up another production company.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: If you don't believe me, just read last week's newspapers. This is all common knowledge!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: If he's going to set up a new studio, why wouldn't he just rehire us to work for that one?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: He's betting on mainstream blockbusters. We make our money from slasher films. There's no room for us in his plans.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: *sigh* Then where will we be able to find any work?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: What are you hanging your head for, Tippi?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: Haven't you worked for several major studio films over the past two years? You've made a name for yourself, so you at least shouldn't have any trouble finding work.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: ...Tippi?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: No, I have to go talk to the boss.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Boss, I want to talk to you about something—
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Tippi, I already explained this very clearly in our last meeting. I don't have a dime to my name.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Obviously, I'd love to pay you if I could, but... the studio's broke. There's no money to give you.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: That's not what I'm here about.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: ...Oh? Then, what, did you just want to say goodbye?
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: I appreciate it, but you might want to hold off on that.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: I'm just shutting this studio down, not leaving the film business for good. I'm sure we'll meet again on another set soon enough.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: No, it's not that either.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Then why do you want to speak to me?
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: I just wanted to know what plans you have for the backgrounds in storage.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: ...The backgrounds?
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: The ones we made for the films. They should be stored in the prop room behind the office.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Oh, right, those. Finance just came to me two days ago and told me that it had cost us 1,530,000 bucks to store all that wood during the past two years.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: 1,530,000 gold certificates. I don't even think it's worth the 30,000 certs it would cost to ship them to the lumber yard.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: So I called somebody to come take care of them. No idea if they'll take them to the lumber yard or chop them up for firewood. Doesn't matter to me.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: But we all invested so much time creating these together... And they hold so many memories for our audience, too.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Who cares about backgrounds made by a feckless film studio?
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Tippi, you're a skilled background painter. Your work in Bloodthirsty Midnight was nothing short of astounding.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: To be honest, when we looked at those, they looked so real we couldn't believe they were actually just some painted wood.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: That's what makes you stand out from the rest. You're the best artist in the crew. People actually like the stuff you make.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: But Bloodthirsty Midnight lost me 30 million bucks. It's the biggest flop I've ever had.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: The screenwriter, the director, the cinematographer, the actors—not one of them was up to the task. What use were your beautifully painted backgrounds then?
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: If the public doesn't like what they see, then we won't make any money, and no more movies will get made. That's just how it works.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Tastes can evolve. After more than ten years making horror films, it's time for me to broaden my audience.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Don't you want more people to recognize your work? If so, then you need to keep aiming higher.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Other people may not know your background, Tippi, but I do. Go ask your grandfather about all this. He'd agree with me.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: If folks had just looked out for the studio and kept up with the times, we wouldn't end up like this.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Well... can I at least keep one of the backgrounds?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: The studio paid you to paint those, filmed them, and distributed tens of thousands of copies of them into the market.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Everything involved in the production is the property of Excelsior Studios, including, of course, the backgrounds.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: You can look through the Columbian IP laws yourself. It's written in black and white.
attributes.name: Film Studio Boss

attributes.content: Everyone, a final toast before we go our separate ways. Tippi, you can have grape juice instead.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Here here.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor & Tippi

attributes.content: C'mon Tippi, don't be so down in the dumps.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: You may not have realized, but plenty of people knew that the boss was actually planning on investing in those 'box office blockbusters'. It was just a matter of when.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: As for that background you wanted... trust me, the ones you're going to paint in the future will all be so much better.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: The heads of the big studios know you're skilled. And they've got plenty of resources, so you'll have plenty of chances to fulfill your dream.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Well, what studios are you guys going to go work for after this?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: I know a few good producers, maybe they could—
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Me? I'll pass. Guess I'll just find some ad agency to work for. Some of them love hiring ex-cinematographers.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Haha. That is, of course, assuming they haven't seen any of my previous works.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: And what about you, Eileen? You've been looking at job ads for six months now. Have you found a new gig yet?
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Ugh. Was I that obvious?!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: I've used the same job website before, so the second I accidently glanced at your screen, I knew.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: I-I see.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: To be honest, I don't know what I'm going to do next...
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: These past four years, all I've done was operate the clapperboard and log shot numbers.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: Now, if you tell me I have to go do some other kind of work, I can't even really picture that. Maybe I'll go back home and study a new trade. Maybe a quiet, stable life would better suit me.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: ...So you're both leaving Wrankwood?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Tippi, I just happened to get into this dirty business a bit sooner than you, but I'm nowhere near as talented or lucky as you.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Being able to make a living as a professional cameraman is more than enough for me. It doesn't much matter if that happens in Wrankwood or not.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: To most of us, working at Excelsior Studios was just a job.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Sometimes you put your all into a job, telling yourself not to quit, only to realize it just wasn't the right fit. And that's perfectly normal.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: So many people come to Wrankwood to 'make it big', but few ever do.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: But I really treasured my time working with all of you!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: I don't think you're any less talented than the folks who do big mainstream flicks—if anything, you've got more heart than those studio types.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Which is why I wanted to stay here and work with you. I really wanted us to shoot a few more films together...
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: I don't see why the boss has to get rid of you like this!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: It's so sweet of you to say so, Tippi, and that's exactly what I'll be missing in the future, not anything else.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: At first, I only came to Wrankwood because people loved my acting on the school play. But once I got here, I discovered I was average at best.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: But you, Tippi, with your talent, you'll definitely write... err... paint your name in Wrankwood history.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: You were born for this job. You need to stay here.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: And if you ever end up on that podium to accept an award, I can already see myself bragging to the folks back home that I once worked with you!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: But... but...
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Hey, does anyone remember we were here to have fun and cuss out the boss together?
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: The bar is empty right now. Why don't we put on a movie, have a few drinks, and chat for a bit, like old times.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Maybe we could let Tippi pick the movie this time?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: Let me think... How about we go with the first film we worked on together? Bad Boy?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: No. I like to think I have some taste. Why would I want to watch my own terrible film?
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: This is our last time together, Matthew. Let her watch what she wants.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: ....Fine. Bad Boy it is then.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: The old, color-cast projector hums as its cooling fan spins. The three of them sit around a small round table, watching the end credits roll slowly.
...Pfft.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Haha... hahahahaha!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: That was so Friedkin cringe! That guy kept on slashing people until he stood at the awards podium!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Why didn't anyone say anything to the screenwriter during filming?! What were they thinking?
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: I... I don't know. Maybe the director just wanted another gory flick. Man. My stomach hurts from laughing so much!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: And... what kind of camera lens were you using, dude? I could see inside that guy's nostrils and count his nose hairs!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: I... I have no idea! We did so many takes! How did that one make it out of the editing room?!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Ugh! That was absolute trash! Hahaha!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: Hahahaha... right! And look at that background! All smeared! I pulled three all-nighters in a row rushing to finish it.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Every time I watch this, I want to reach inside the screen and scrub my name from the credits.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Wait, you've actually watched this multiple times? I don't think I've seen it even once!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: Tippi, do you actually enjoy watching your own movies at home?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: N-no! I just really like the last scene of this one.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: That ridiculous final scene? Hahahaa.....
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: I like the background in it. That rough-looking podium.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Maybe you don't remember, but that background... the entire cast and crew signed it on the back.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Wait... really?
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: Yeah. This was the first movie I worked on here. Back then they were already saying the studio was about to go under.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: The original cast we had was afraid the studio wouldn't make payroll, and quit right before we started shooting. Then the investors all pulled out.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Yeah, I remember now. We all thought that would be our last movie. It felt like just finishing it was a victory in itself.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: If I remember correctly, I snagged the best-looking extra and turned him into our impromptu leading man.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: And when the filming was finally finished, we signed that background together.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: We still made it, right? And the movie actually did pretty well at the box office—some theaters even put us in daytime slots for the first time!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: We were forced to add those extra gory scenes to cater to our audience, but we never thought they'd love it so much.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: We kept the studio running for two years before it shut down... Still, we did save it once. We're not failures!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: So... you still want us to stick around?
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: I know you're trying to cheer us up, Tippi, but there's no saving the studio this time around.
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: No, it's not that. I respect your decision.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: It's just, I just... before we all part ways, I want to see the signatures on that background one last time.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: ...Are you coming with me?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: A weak glow reveals a huge pile of prop, covered with dust. Once filmed, they were all left here untouched.
*cough cough cough cough* It's so dusty in here! I can barely breathe!
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: (whispers) Keep it down, Matthew! There are guards at the gate. We can't let them know we're here!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: (covers mouth) Hrrgh....ugh.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: (whispers) All of these backgrounds piled up are taller than me several times over... I never realized we'd made so many films!
attributes.name: Script Supervisor

attributes.content: (whispers) Hah! You know what's more unbelievable? If you just stacked your shot logs and my reels together they'd be taller than either of us.
attributes.name: Cinematographer

attributes.content: (whispers) Hey! I found it!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: The three of them walk up to a background. It's been two years, but its paint still looks fresh.
Let's flip it over.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Wow. Our glorious achievement.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: This 'Final Movie' wasn't our final one. We saved this place.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: These mountains of backgrounds are proof of all the work we did together. They could easily pave the entire Walk of Fame.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: (sobs)
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: Matthew... are you alright?
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: I'm... I'm fine. I'm just... a little drunk.
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: The big man forces his sobs down as Eileen's eyes swell up with tears.
Tippi takes her friends' hands and slowly walks toward the background.
Just like the story of Bad Boy, we've now busted out of prison, wiped out all the villains in the hall, and stepped onto the podium—
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: And now we have Wrankwood's Best Cinematographer, Mr. Matthew Thomas! And Wrankwood's Best Script Supervisor, Miss Eileen Hunter! Would you two like to say a few words?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Err...
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: (shouting) Screw you, Excelsior Studios!
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: (shouting even louder) Eat shit, Wrankwood!
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: Uh... Screw you, Excelsior Studios!
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: Eat shit, Wrankwood!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Their voices linger in the warehouse for a long time. Only after the echoes completely fade do the three of them burst into uncontrollable laughter.
Hahaha. I'm drunk. We're all drunk!
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: Just now, all our years of filming movies together flashed through my mind. Time really flies!
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: Yeah. It's like a dream.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Tippi grabs tightly to Matthew and Eileen's hands and slowly raises their arms.
My friends, now it is time for us to close the final curtain.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: (Raises arm)
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: (Raises arm)
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: If only we had a spotlight...
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Before Tippi can finish speaking, a sharp beam of light hits the three of them, so bright they cannot even keep their eyes open.
Who's there? Don't move!
attributes.name: Security Guard

attributes.content: Hands up!
attributes.name: Security Guard

attributes.content: Damn. We were a bit too loud. Should we run?
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: ...We have to!
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: Wait!
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: Wh-What's wrong?
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: They're coming!
attributes.name: Eileen

attributes.content: Well, now that we've been spotted, might as well take this podium with us, right?
attributes.name: Matthew

attributes.content: Tippi takes a deep breath. She can sense the beam of light growing in intensity as it nears them.
I like the way you think.
attributes.name: Tippi

attributes.content: That night, a giant background, with a huge podium painted on it, rolled through the streets of Wrankwood.
Even in Wrankwood, where strange happenings are aplenty, a scene like this was surreal. People gathered around, taking photos of it.
People took shot of the podium passing Wrankwood's Walk of Fame, the Hall of Fame, and the Film Plaza...
But, for some unknown reason, no one got a clear shot of the people carrying it. There were just three vague, shadowy outlines in those photos.
Fortunately, the public didn't look too closely into the identities of these three, and the entire matter became fodder for dinner table conversations.
After all, so many people come to Wrankwood to 'make it big', but few ever do.
